metadata.claim_text: Patellar instability is a tracking and soft-tissue restraint problem whose recurrence risk guides MPFL and bony procedure discussion.
Apprehension and J-sign suggest lateral patellar tracking and instability.
Rehabilitation milestones and return-to-play criteria for patellar instability require attending-defined progression gates.
Do not anchor on a single exam maneuver for patellar instability — integrate mechanism, instability symptoms, and imaging pattern.
Patellar Instability pathoanatomy centers on patella, extensor-mechanism, femoral-condyles and related stabilizers.
State mechanism, key exam findings, imaging role, treatment concept, and return-to-play framework for patellar instability.

metadata.description: Lateral elbow stabilizer resisting varus stress.

metadata.action: Discuss operative reconstruction versus continued rehabilitation; graft selection and fixation are attending decisions
